title: Photos: Stunning views of Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The USS Constitution led over 60 international tall ships and military vessels into Boston Harbor for Sail Boston 250.
The ships, from more than 20 countries, are docked at various locations in Boston for the six-day event.
Thousands visited Boston to witness the ships, marking the final stop of the Sail250 tour.

title: Red Sox draft SS Jake Schaffner with No. 20 pick
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox pick UNC shortstop Jake Schaffner at No. 20 in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Ranked 75th by MLB.com, Schaffner hit .356 with six home runs and 50 RBI in 2026.
Schaffner recorded a .987 fielding percentage with 26 stolen bases and eight triples this season.

title: Sail Boston 2026: The Grand Parade of Sail is rooted in rich history and tradition in the Commonwealth
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: The Sail Boston 2026 Grand Parade of Sail was led by the USS Constitution, showcasing historic ships.
The event features both replica and original schooners, including Bluenose Two and Spirit of Bermuda, celebrating maritime history.
Spectators line the city from Castle Island to Piers Park, marking the 250th anniversary with a multi-generational celebration.

title: Brayan Bello named midseason AL ‘Cy Yuk’ winner by The Athletic for poor first-half performance
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Brayan Bello of the Boston Red Sox was awarded the AL 'Cy Yuk' by The Athletic for a poor first-half performance.
Bello recorded a 10.35 ERA over eight starts, allowing 41 earned runs in 35.2 innings before being sent to Triple-A.
Despite poor starting performance, Bello excelled as a reliever with a 0.71 ERA and 0.91 WHIP in 25.1 relief innings.

title: Red Sox face all too familiar question heading into MLB trade deadline: Buy or sell?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Boston Red Sox are considering whether to buy or sell at the 2026 MLB trade deadline.
Boston is on a seven-game winning streak and is less than two games from an AL wild-card spot.
Craig Breslow's decisions could impact his job security and determine Boston's playoff chances.

title: Review & Setlist: Shakira delivers high-energy nostalgia and new beginnings at TD Garden
author: Maddie Browning
bullets: Shakira performed at TD Garden as part of her 'Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran' World Tour, featuring a mix of nostalgia and new songs.
The concert on July 10, 2026, featured a dramatic stage setup with elements like an inflatable she-wolf and laser effects.
Shakira's setlist included hits like 'Hips Don’t Lie' and 'Waka Waka,' while showcasing her diverse dance and musical talents.

title: Healey announces tax credit awards to build almost 700 new homes in six Mass. cities
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Gov. Healey announced $18.6 million in tax credits to develop 662 new homes in six Massachusetts Gateway Cities.
The projects will convert historic buildings in Fall River, Lawrence, Lowell, Pittsfield, Taunton, and Worcester into housing.
Massachusetts tripled the Housing Development Incentive Program's annual budget to $30 million to boost housing in Gateway Cities.

title: FBI arrests Mass. man in armed bank robberies in NH, NJ
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Joseph Sawyer, 52, was arrested by an FBI SWAT team in Albany, New York, for two armed bank robberies in NH and NJ.
Sawyer allegedly used a semiautomatic handgun to rob St. Mary’s Bank in Nashua, NH, and a Chase Bank in Boonton, NJ.
The FBI's arrest of Sawyer was part of Operation Summer Heat 2.0, a collaboration involving multiple states and federal agencies.

title: Motorcyclist missing since July 4 found dead in NH
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Wesley Grondin, 41, was found dead with his motorcycle near Route 2 in Shelburne, NH, after missing since July 4.
He was discovered by a resident searching for him; police report he had crossed into the opposite lane before the crash.
No other vehicles were involved; both Berlin Police and Shelburne Fire assisted. Investigation into the crash continues.

title: Gov. Maura Healey declares July 11 ‘Noah Kahan Day’
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey declares July 11, 2026, as 'Noah Kahan Day' in Massachusetts, honoring the singer's contributions.
Noah Kahan headlines four consecutive sold-out concerts at Fenway Park, attracting over 150,000 fans in total.
Kahan acknowledged for his advocacy on mental health, highlighted by his Netflix documentary 'Out of Body' and nonprofit work.
